description: Fix bugs, resource leaks, and missing guards found during a systematic audit of the MCP server backend code.

userStories.title: Clear activeNetworkCaptures and tabMapping on extension disconnect
userStories.description: As a platform operator, I want stale state to be cleaned up when the extension disconnects so that the server does not report phantom network captures or stale tab states after reconnection.
acceptanceCriteria: createHandleWsClose clears state.activeNetworkCaptures (call .clear()) when the disconnecting ws matches state.extensionWs
createHandleWsClose clears state.tabMapping (call .clear()) when the disconnecting ws matches state.extensionWs
Existing behavior preserved: pendingDispatches and pendingConfirmations are still rejected on disconnect
Unit test in http-routes.test.ts verifies activeNetworkCaptures and tabMapping are cleared on matching ws close
userStories.notes: File: platform/mcp-server/src/http-routes.ts, lines 593-614 (createHandleWsClose function). Currently only rejects pendingDispatches and pendingConfirmations. Add state.activeNetworkCaptures.clear() and state.tabMapping.clear() inside the `if (state.extensionWs === ws)` block, after the existing cleanup. The activeNetworkCaptures set tracks tab IDs with active debugger sessions — if the extension disconnects, those sessions are gone. The tabMapping tracks plugin-to-tab state — stale entries cause the server to dispatch to tabs that may no longer exist. Add a test case in platform/mcp-server/src/http-routes.test.ts (look for existing test 'matching ws rejects all pending dispatches') that populates activeNetworkCaptures and tabMapping before calling wsClose, then asserts both are empty.

userStories.title: Log post-startup HTTP server errors instead of silently swallowing them
userStories.description: As a platform operator, I want HTTP server errors that occur after startup to be logged so that I can diagnose issues like file descriptor exhaustion or socket errors.
acceptanceCriteria: httpServer.on('error') handler logs the error via log.error (or console.error) in addition to calling rejectServer
The log message includes the error code and message
Pre-startup errors still reject the server promise as before
userStories.notes: File: platform/mcp-server/src/server-node.ts, lines 326-328. The error handler only calls rejectServer(err), which is a no-op after the server promise has resolved. Add a log statement (import log from ./logger.js) so post-startup errors are at least visible in server logs. The fix is simply adding a log line before the rejectServer call — e.g., log.error('HTTP server error:', err.code, err.message). The rejectServer call is harmless (no-op after resolution) so keep it for the pre-startup case.

userStories.title: Fix Vue version lost in analyze-site framework deduplication
userStories.description: As a plugin developer using analyze-site, I want Vue version information to be preserved so that the site analysis report shows the actual Vue version instead of undefined.
acceptanceCriteria: FRAMEWORK_PROBE_SCRIPT deduplication prefers entries with a version over entries without a version for the same framework name
When both window.__VUE__ and __VUE_DEVTOOLS_GLOBAL_HOOK__ are present, the version from the devtools hook is preserved in the output
Deduplication still removes true duplicates (same name, same version)

Unit test in detect-framework.test.ts (or analyze-site test) verifies that a probes array with [{name:'vue',version:undefined},{name:'vue',version:'3.4.1'}] deduplicates to [{name:'vue',version:'3.4.1'}]
userStories.notes: File: platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/analyze-site/index.ts, lines 203-212. The comment says 'keep first occurrence with version' but the code just keeps the first occurrence regardless. The dedup loop should prefer an entry with a defined version. Fix: change the dedup logic so that if a name is already seen but the existing entry has no version and the new entry does, replace it. For example: track entries in a Map<string, probe> instead of a Set, and when a duplicate name is found with a version where the existing one has none, overwrite it. The FRAMEWORK_PROBE_SCRIPT is a string template that runs in the browser — keep it concise. The Vue probe pushes versionless first (line 162) then versioned (line 167), so the dedup must handle this order.

userStories.title: Deep-freeze EMPTY_REGISTRY to prevent accidental mutation
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want the EMPTY_REGISTRY sentinel to be truly immutable so that accidental .set() calls on its maps produce runtime errors instead of silently corrupting shared state.
acceptanceCriteria: Attempting to call .set() on any of the frozen maps throws a TypeError
Existing code that replaces state.registry with a new registry still works (the new registry's maps are NOT frozen)

userStories.title: Add try-catch around deleteExecFile in execute-script finally block
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want consistent error handling for temp file cleanup so that a deleteExecFile failure does not swallow the original dispatch error.
acceptanceCriteria: execute-script.ts finally block wraps deleteExecFile call in try-catch, matching the pattern used in analyze-site/index.ts
If deleteExecFile throws, the error is logged but does not replace the original dispatch result or error
userStories.notes: File: platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/execute-script.ts, lines 49-51. Currently: `finally { await deleteExecFile(filename); }`. Change to: `finally { try { await deleteExecFile(filename); } catch { /* best-effort cleanup */ } }`. Compare with platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/analyze-site/index.ts lines 83-87 which already uses this pattern. The deleteExecFile function in adapter-files.ts currently has its own try-catch, but the execute-script caller should not rely on that implementation detail.

userStories.title: Remove unhandled plugin.uninstall response and misleading removeLocalPlugin error
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want clean protocol semantics so that fire-and-forget messages use notifications (no id) and config write failures surface the real error message.
acceptanceCriteria: handlePluginRemove sends plugin.uninstall as a JSON-RPC notification (no id field) instead of a request
removeLocalPlugin propagates config write errors to the caller instead of silently returning removed=false
If atomicWrite fails during removeLocalPlugin, the error message reaches the MCP client (not 'Plugin not found')
userStories.notes:
Two fixes in this story:

1. File: platform/mcp-server/src/extension-handlers.ts, lines 582-587. Change the plugin.uninstall message from a request (has id: getNextRequestId()) to a notification (no id field). The extension's response is never handled anyway. Remove the `id` property from the JSON-RPC object.

2. File: platform/mcp-server/src/plugin-management.ts, lines 361-441. The .catch() on line 435 catches atomicWrite errors and logs them, but sets removed=false. The caller then returns 'Plugin not found in localPlugins config' which is misleading. Fix: re-throw the error from the catch block so it propagates to the caller (removePlugin), which already has a try-catch that sends proper error responses. Alternatively, set an error variable in the catch block and check it after awaiting the mutex.
